Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts history, breaks 20-year-old record with fastest fifty in List A cricket
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Pic credit: BCCI)

Indian teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has etched his name into the record books by registering the fastest fifty in List A cricket history during the Tri-Nation Series final between India A and Sri Lanka A at the Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium.The 15-year-old opener unleashed a breathtaking assault on the Sri Lankan bowlers after Sri Lanka A elected to field first, racing to his half-century in just 11 deliveries, conjuring up a stunning sequence of 4, 4, 4, 6, 6, 0, 6, 4, 4, 6, 6 off his first 11 deliveries. In doing so, Sooryavanshi shattered the previous world record of a 12-ball fifty, set by Sri Lanka's Kaushalya Weeraratne in 2005.Here are the top 5 fastest fifty in list A cricket history:The historic moment came when Sooryavanshi launched left-arm seamer Dulaj Samuditha straight down the ground for his fifth six of the innings, bringing up a remarkable fifty and rewriting the List A record books.In the third over the left-hander clobbered 26 runs off Mohamed Shiraz’s over. After failing to cross the 50-run mark throughout the competition (14, 44, 21 and 38), Sooryavanshi stepped up big time in the final and eventually ended with a 29-ball 94, to lay a solid foundation for India A in the Ti Nation final at Dambulla.Priyansh Arya, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Ruturaj Gaikwad, Tilak Varma (c), Kumar Kushagra (wk), Suryansh Shedge, Nishant Sindhu, Anukul Roy, Vipraj Nigam, Ashok Sharma, Yash Thakur.Niroshan Dickwella (wk), Avishka Fernando, Nuwanidu Fernando, Sadeera Samarawickrama, Sahan Arachchige (c), Ravindu Fernando, Wanuja Sahan, Mohamed Shiraz, Vijayakanth Viyaskanth, Dulaj Samuditha, Kugathas Mathulan.
Article Summary: Vaibhav sooryavanshi scores fastest fifty in list a cricket history. The 15-year-old hit 94 off just 29 balls in the final of the tri-nation series final between india a and sri lanka a at dambulla. He broke the previous world record of a 12-ball fifty set by sri lanka's kaushalya weeraratne in 2005.

Article Title: Shubman Gill breaks silence on Rohit Sharma's ODI future: 'It is a good kind of headache'
Article Text:
Rohit Sharma (BCCI Photo)

TimesofIndia.com in Chennai:

With Yashasvi Jaiswal scoring his second century in the last three ODIs, the pressure is mounting on the selectors to decide the future of veteran Rohit Sharma . Although the 39-year-old survived a jittery start in the third ODI, he went on to score an eye-catching 79 and put on 170 runs for the opening wicket with Yashasvi Jaiswal.Rohit, who got a reprieve early in his innings, took Afghanistan spinner Rashid Khan to the cleaners before getting dismissed. In the three-match series, his scores are 16, 48 and 79. But are those knocks enough for him to secure his place for the upcoming England tour? And if he does, where does that leave the 24-year-old youngster, who, despite his stop-start ODI career, has been in rollicking form and has tried to maximise every opportunity he has got?"It is a good kind of headache to have when all your players are performing," captain Shubman Gill told reporters when he was asked about India's opening combination for the upcoming England tour."The squad will be announced either tomorrow or in the next couple of days, so we will look at the squad and then put out the best XI in England."When asked where Jaiswal stands if everyone is fit, Gill said: "We will have to see where everyone's fitness stands.If everyone is fit, as I said, we will formulate the best possible XI based on that."However, the skipper had special praise for Jaiswal and called it "unfortunate" that he has not been getting a long rope in ODIs."We all know he's a phenomenal player. It's not easy for any player because, when everyone is available, he's unfortunately the one who sometimes misses out," said Gill."Since Virat bhai wasn't available in this series, he got the opportunity to play a couple of games, and today he played really well. Hopefully, he'll continue this form and keep grabbing the opportunities he gets.""I think a lot of the boxes are ticked," he told broadcasters at the post-match presentation."We spoke about keeping the intensity in the middle overs, and one of the things we also discussed was how we can keep creating opportunities in the middle overs as a bowling unit and, as a batting unit, how we can keep pushing for extra runs in the middle overs."Going to England, the conditions will be a little more similar to what we get in South Africa. Not quite the same, but still close. So the kind of combination that we would be looking to play there, hopefully with all the players fit and ready, should make it another great series for us," he said.
Article Summary: Yashasvi jaiswal scored his second century in the last three odis. The pressure is mounting on the selectors to decide the future of rohit sharma. The 39-year-old survived a jittery start in the third odi.

Article Title: Yashasvi Jaiswal leaves Virat Kohli, Shubman Gill behind in major ODI record
Article Text:
Yashasvi Jaiswal (BCCI Photo)

Fewest innings by an Indian to score two ODI hundreds



6 - Yashasvi Jaiswal

7 - Shikhar Dhawan

9 - Kedar Jadhav

17 - Virat Kohli

18 - Shubman Gill

Fastest ODI centuries against Afghanistan (by balls faced)



57 - Eoin Morgan (2019)

63 - Rohit Sharma (2023)

71 - Ishan Kishan (2026)

76 - Glenn Maxwell (2023)

77 - Shubman Gill (2026)

83 - William Porterfield (2017)

83 - Yashasvi Jaiswal (2026)

NEW DELHI: Yashasvi Jaiswal continued his impressive start to ODI cricket with a match-winning century against Afghanistan in Chennai on Saturday, achieving a milestone faster than Indian stars Virat Kohli and Shubman Gill The left-handed opener scored an unbeaten 110 as India thrashed Afghanistan by nine wickets in the third ODI to complete a 3-0 series sweep. The hundred was Jaiswal's second in ODIs and it came in just his sixth innings, making him the fastest Indian to reach two ODI centuries.Jaiswal's achievement puts him ahead of some of India's biggest ODI names, including Kohli and Gill.Chasing 219, India were always in control. They began with an unusual score of 5/0 after Afghanistan were penalised five runs during their innings.Jaiswal wasted no time and attacked from the start. He hit three boundaries in the opening over and reached his fifty in just 38 balls. Rohit Sharma also looked in fine touch and completed his half-century in 47 deliveries.The duo added 170 runs for the opening wicket in only 22.5 overs.While Rohit entertained with his trademark strokes, Jaiswal played with confidence throughout the innings. He struck a six off Fareed Ahmad and played some elegant drives against the spinners.Rohit eventually fell for 79, but Jaiswal carried on and reached his century in 83 balls. The knock also placed him among the fastest players to score an ODI hundred against Afghanistan.Interestingly, three of the seven fastest ODI hundreds against Afghanistan have come during this series.Earlier, Prasidh Krishna starred with the ball, claiming a career-best 5/23 as Afghanistan were bowled out for 218. Captain Hashmatullah Shahidi fought hard with a maiden ODI century, scoring 102 after his side had slumped to 36/4.But Jaiswal's sparkling hundred ensured there were no surprises in the chase as India sealed another comfortable victory and completed a clean sweep of the series.
Article Summary: Yashasvi jaiswal scores unbeaten 110 as india thrash afghanistan by nine wickets in the third odi to complete a 3-0 series sweep. The hundred was jaiswalk's second in odis and it came in just his sixth innings, making him the fastest indian to reach two odi centuries.

Article Title: No way through Room: Curacao goalkeeper Eloy makes record for most saves in a 90-minute match as Curacao earn first-ever FIFA World Cup point
Article Text:
Ecuador's Enner Valencia (13) shoots at goal as Curaçao goalkeeper Eloy Room defends during the World Cup Group E football match between Ecuador and Curacao in Kansas City, Mo., Saturday, June 20, 2026. (AP)

Curacao goalkeeper Eloy Room produced a performance to remember as his side held Ecuador to a 0-0 draw in their FIFA World Cup Group E match on Saturday (local time), earning the island nation its first-ever point at the tournament.The result also confirmed Germany's place in the knockout stage after their 2-1 win over Ivory Coast earlier in the day.Room, 37, made 15 saves against Ecuador and was named FIFA Man of the Match. His tally is the highest ever recorded in a 90-minute World Cup match and just one short of the overall World Cup record of 16 saves, set by United States goalkeeper Tim Howard against Belgium on July 1, 2014, since saves became an official statistic in 1966.The Curacao goalkeeper had helped his country qualify for its first World Cup by keeping a clean sheet against Jamaica last November. After conceding seven goals in a 7-1 defeat to Germany earlier in the tournament, he responded with one of the most memorable goalkeeping displays seen on the World Cup stage.The draw keeps both teams in contention for a place in the knockout rounds, but it leaves Ecuador under pressure heading into the final group match.On Thursday, Curacao will face Ivory Coast in Philadelphia, while Ecuador will take on Germany in New York.Ecuador enjoyed strong support in the stands at the home of the Kansas City Chiefs. Fans wearing yellow filled most of the stadium, while only a few sections were occupied by Curacao supporters, news agency AP reported.Ecuador entered the game knowing defeat could seriously damage their hopes after Germany's late win over Ivory Coast. Curacao ensured that pressure remained throughout the contest.In the first half, Curacao repeatedly found space through the middle of Ecuador's defence and created openings, but poor final passes and off-target finishing prevented them from taking the lead.Ecuador's best chance of the opening period fell to veteran striker Enner Valencia. With only the goalkeeper to beat, he looked set to score, but Room guessed correctly, dived to his left and pushed the effort away.Ecuador increased the pressure after the break, but Room continued to deny them.Moises Caicedo forced the goalkeeper into a save early in the second half. Valencia then came close with a header, but Room pushed it wide. From the resulting corner, the Curacao goalkeeper made two more saves before his defence cleared the danger.It was a frustrating night for Ecuador, who could not find a way past Room despite creating numerous chances.For Curacao, however, the draw delivered a historic moment, with the team securing its first point at a FIFA World Cup and keeping its hopes of progressing alive heading into the final group game.
Article Summary: Curacao goalkeeper eloy room produced a performance to remember as his side held ecuador to a 0-0 draw in their fifa world cup group e match. Room, 37, made 15 saves against ecuador and was named fifa man of the match. His tally is the highest ever recorded in a 90-minute world cup match and just one short of the overall world cup record of 16 saves.

Article Title: 'Lionel Messi deserved a red card': Algeria complain to FIFA over controversial tackle in World Cup, question VAR
Article Text:
Argentina's Lionel Messi and Algeria's Rayan Ait-Nouri battle for the ball during the World Cup Group J football match between Argentina and Algeria in Kansas City, Mo., Tuesday, June 16, 2026. (AP Photo)

Algeria have filed a complaint with FIFA over what they described as "refereeing injustice" during their opening FIFA World Cup defeat to Argentina, news agency AFP reported, quoting an Algerian football federation source.The complaint centres on an incident involving Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who caught Algeria defender Aissa Mandi on the calf with a studs-up challenge from behind in the 30th minute of Tuesday's match but was not shown a card.Messi had already put Argentina ahead before the challenge and later went on to score a hat-trick. Algeria supporters called for the forward to be sent off after the tackle on Mandi, but no disciplinary action was taken and Messi remained on the field."The complaint primarily concerns Messi's challenge, which deserved a red card, in everyone's opinion," the federation source was quoted as saying by AFP."There were also two elbow incidents... Both of which warranted dismissals.""We are not saying that the Argentine team was not strong, but we cannot remain silent in the face of injustice," the source added, as quoted by AFP."There were three incidents that were crystal clear, and VAR did not intervene."Messi's hat-trick saw him draw level with Miroslav Klose as the joint-highest scorer in World Cup history with 16 goals.Algeria will face Jordan in their second Group J match on Monday before taking on Austria on June 27. Argentina, meanwhile, will play Austria in Dallas on Monday.Algeria are aiming to reach the World Cup knockout stage for only the second time in their history, having previously advanced to the last 16 in 2014 before losing in extra time to eventual champions Germany.
Article Summary: Algeria have filed a complaint with fifa over what they described as "refereeing injustice" during their opening fifa world cup defeat to argentina. The complaint centres on an incident involving argentina captain lionel messi. Messi caught algeria defender aissa mandi on the calf with a studs-up challenge from behind but was not shown a card.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026: USMNT's brightest star only plays for America because of a delayed flight
Article Text:
USMNT star Folarin Balogun's career could have looked very different without one family trip/ Image: X

The trip to New York that changed everything



England prospect, Arsenal academy star and international tug-of-war

United States' Folarin Balogun, centre, is congratulated by teammates after scoring his team's second goal during the World Cup Group D soccer match between the United States and Paraguay in Inglewood, Calif., near Los Angeles, Friday, June 12, 2026. (AP Photo/Mark J. Terrill)

The Reims breakthrough that changed everything

Why Balogun chose the United States

The immigration debate surrounding Balogun's citizenship

Pulisic and former stars praise Balogun's impact

Folarin Balogun announced himself on the biggest stage in football when he scored twice in the United States' opening FIFA World Cup 2026 victory over Paraguay. Days later, he followed that performance with another influential display against Australia, helping force the own goal that set Mauricio Pochettino's side on their way to a 2-0 victory and qualification for the Round of 32.The 24-year-old has quickly become one of the faces of the United States' World Cup campaign. Yet his journey to representing the USMNT is unlike almost any other player at the tournament.Balogun grew up in London, developed through Arsenal's academy system and represented England throughout his youth career. He was also eligible to play for Nigeria through his parents. Yet today he leads the line for the United States, the country he left when he was only weeks old.That opportunity exists because of a remarkable chain of events that began before he was born.Balogun was born in Brooklyn, New York, on July 3, 2001. Under American birthright citizenship laws, anyone born on United States soil automatically receives US citizenship. That legal status would ultimately make him eligible to represent the United States internationally.The unusual aspect of the story is that Balogun's family never intended for him to be born there.His mother, Florence, travelled from London to New York while seven months pregnant to visit family. The original plan was to return home before the birth, but circumstances intervened.Speaking previously to ESPN , Florence explained how events unfolded."Where the story came from that he was in America until he was 2, I'll never know," she said."I had him in July and by the end of August we were back in London."She revealed that airline staff refused to allow her to board a flight home because she was too advanced in her pregnancy."My belly was so massive," she said.Without the required documentation from her doctor in London, Florence remained in New York and stayed with family in Brooklyn until she gave birth.The experience left a lasting impression on her."I don't believe things happened by luck," she told ESPN."I think for me to have gone to America and for me to have had him there, it is just something that has really stuck with me. Even when he wasn't even thinking of making an international decision, I'd already made up my mind that he is going to play for America."Within two months of his birth, the family returned to London, where Balogun would spend virtually his entire upbringing.Balogun joined Arsenal's academy at the age of eight and quickly established himself as one of the club's most highly regarded attacking prospects.During his development years he represented both England and the United States at youth level, although he became most closely associated with England's youth setup.Under Lee Carsley, he excelled for England Under-21s.Across 13 appearances, Balogun scored seven goals, including six goals in five matches during European Under-21 Championship qualifying.At that stage, many expected his senior international future to lie with England.Nigeria also remained a realistic option through his family heritage, creating a three-way international battle for his services.While he made ten first-team appearances for Arsenal, consistent opportunities proved difficult to secure. A mixed loan spell at Middlesbrough followed before his career exploded during a loan move to France.Balogun's breakthrough arrived during the 2022-23 season with Reims. Playing in Ligue 1, the striker enjoyed one of the most productive campaigns by a young forward anywhere in Europe. He scored 21 goals in 37 league matches and emerged as one of the continent's most sought-after attacking talents.The performances immediately attracted attention from United States Soccer.The USMNT launched an aggressive recruitment effort as they sought the long-term successor to legendary forwards such as Clint Dempsey and Landon Donovan.According to reports, the federation rolled out the red carpet during its pursuit. Balogun was even given tickets to attend an NBA game between the Orlando Magic and New York Knicks as discussions intensified.In May 2023, FIFA formally approved his one-time switch of international allegiance from England to the United States.Three months later he completed a permanent transfer from Arsenal to AS Monaco.Despite external influences, Balogun has consistently maintained that the final decision was his alone.Speaking to ESPN, he explained the role his family played throughout the process."She's a big believer that you should go to where your heart is desired," Balogun said of his mother."For her, it was really heartwarming just how much [the American fans] wanted to support me, and how much they wanted me to be a part of it. That almost came onto me as well."He added:"But in the end, it wasn't about that. It was about me naturally just feeling that this is the best thing, and that this is what I wanted to do."Since making his senior United States debut in June 2023, Balogun has rapidly become a central figure within the national team.Before the Australia match he had accumulated 28 caps and 11 international goals. His appearance against the Socceroos took him to 29 caps, while his contribution helped secure a victory that officially sent the United States into the Round of 32.Balogun's story has also generated wider discussion beyond football. His American citizenship exists because he was born on US soil while his parents were visiting on a temporary visa.Under proposals previously supported by President Donald Trump, birthright citizenship rules would be significantly restricted for future cases involving temporary visitors and certain non-citizen residents.Had such rules existed in 2001, Balogun may not have automatically received American citizenship at birth.The irony is striking. One of the biggest stars in the current USMNT squad might never have been eligible to represent the United States under the proposed changes.Balogun's performances at the World Cup have only reinforced belief that the United States secured one of the most important international recruitment victories in recent memory.After the opening victory, captain Christian Pulisic summed up what many supporters were thinking."The kid's insane," Pulisic said."He's lethal right now in front of goal. Let's just hope it keeps going like this."Former United States international Kenny Cooper offered a similarly glowing assessment while speaking to the BBC."He's obviously a really special talent and he showed that with two exceptional goals," Cooper said."He has been so impressive."Cooper added:"I think there's just so much confidence that I'm sure the players have in him playing with them, and us, his fans, have in him."For the United States, Balogun has become the striker they spent years searching for. For the player himself, the journey began with a delayed flight, an unexpected birth in Brooklyn and a citizenship that ultimately altered the course of his international career. More than two decades later, that twist of fate has helped place him at the centre of America's hopes at FIFA World Cup 2026.
Article Summary: Usmnt star folarin balogun's career could have looked very different without one family trip to new york that changed everything. Arsenal academy star and international tug-of-war with nigeria and england. Balogun made his senior united states debut in june 2023. Before the world cup he had accumulated 28 caps and 11 international goals.

Article Title: Brazil president Lula mocks Neymar, calls him 'home office player' - here's why
Article Text:
Brazil president Lula and Neymar

Brazil President Lula took a light-hearted dig at Neymar over the forward’s injury problems during the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026.Speaking at an event, Lula asked a young boy who he thought was Brazil’s best player. The child quickly answered Neymar, prompting the president to joke about the star’s absence from the field.“Neymar isn’t even playing, dude. Neymar is the world’s first home office call-up. Home office player," Lula said. “I saw that on the internet yesterday. Any day now, they’re gonna have to make a National Team with Artificial Intelligence. 11 Pelés."Meanwhile, Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti has confirmed that Neymar will be available for the team's crucial Group C match against Scotland, giving the five-time champions a major boost in their bid to reach the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout stage.The 34-year-old has not played in the tournament after suffering a right calf injury while representing Santos in May. He missed Brazil's 1-1 draw with Morocco and the 3-0 victory over Haiti, but Ancelotti revealed that the forward is nearing full fitness."Neymar will be training tomorrow alone, and on Monday with the team. He will be available for the match against Scotland," Ancelotti said.Neymar has been following an individual recovery program and has yet to take part in a full training session with Brazil.His return has become even more important after winger Raphinha suffered a suspected hamstring injury during the win over Haiti."We need to evaluate him. We don't know the severity yet," Ancelotti said.Brazil bounced back from criticism after the Morocco draw with a convincing win over Haiti, powered by two goals from Matheus Cunha and one from Vinicius Junior.Neymar's comeback could provide both experience and morale for the Seleção as qualification remains on the line ahead of Wednesday's clash in Miami Gardens.
Article Summary: Brazil coach carlo ancelotti confirms neymar will be available for the team's crucial group c match against scotland. The 34-year-old has not played in the tournament after suffering a right calf injury while representing santos in may. He missed brazil's 1-1 draw with morocco and the 3-0 victory over haiti.

Article Title: “MMA GOAT choices are garbage”: Fans brutally troll UFC legend Khabib Nurmagomedov for his FIFA World Cup 2026 winner predictions
Article Text:
Khabib Nurmagomedov (Getty Images)

Khabib Nurmagomedov FIFA World Cup predictions trigger debate among fans



Khabib Nurmagomedov has shared his predictions for the FIFA World Cup 2026. The former UFC lightweight champion, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest mixed martial artists of all time, remains a passionate football follower. As the World Cup continues, Nurmagomedov offered his thoughts on the teams most likely to lift the trophy. He also reflected on the players he expects to shine during the tournament.His comments quickly gained attention on social media. While many fans appreciated hearing his football opinions, others questioned his selections based on the performances seen so far in the competition. The discussion grew even bigger after his predictions were shared online, with supporters and critics offering different views on the likely World Cup winner and the race for the Golden Boot.Speaking during a recent interview, Khabib Nurmagomedov identified Germany as the strongest contender to win the World Cup. Although he admitted he was not personally supporting the German side, he explained that he believes they currently have the best chance of becoming champions. He ranked the Netherlands as his second choice and Spain as his third favorite for the title.The Dagestani star also named several players he expects to compete for the tournament’s top-scorer award. According to Nurmagomedov, stars such as Kylian Mbappe , Lautaro Martinez, Vinicius Junior, Joao Felix, and Harry Kane have the ability to finish among the leading goalscorers. He also revealed that his personal support is with Uzbekistan, praising the team’s determination and hunger to succeed on football’s biggest stage.After the interview clips appeared on social media, many fans responded with mixed reactions. A fan wrote, “how does he know so much about football but his top 2 favourites are absolute garbage”, one wrote, “he’s still stuck in the 90s”. A third one wrote, “Sorry. But the mma Goat doesn't know much about football”. One fan wrote, “And he’s wrong on all of none of them are leading scorers”.This is not the first time Nurmagomedov has spoken about football. Last year, he also shared his opinion on the long-running debate between Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo . While acknowledging the achievements of both legends, he explained that Messi’s success, particularly his World Cup triumph with Argentina, made it difficult to choose anyone else as the greatest player. Nurmagomedov said Messi’s accomplishments speak for themselves and are impossible to overlook.Known for his love of football since childhood, Nurmagomedov continues to follow the sport closely.
Article Summary: Khabib nurmagomedov has shared his predictions for the fifa world cup 2026. The former ufc lightweight champion believes germany will win the tournament. He also named several players he expects to compete for the tournament's top-scorer award. His comments quickly gained attention on social media.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El rubius was eliminated from mrbeast's $1 million challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

RCB register their second win

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal challengers bengaluru (rcb) beat chennai super kings (csk) by 43 runs. Tim david, rajat patidar and devdutt padikkal all scored half-centuries. Rcb set a new record for highest total in ipl 2026 with 250/3.

Article Title: Swedish proverb of the day: 'When you finish the game, the king and pawn end up in the same box.' A timeless lesson on power, humility, mortality and why status means less than character
Article Text:
A Pawn can also defeat a King in a game of Chess

"Quando finisce la partita il re ed il pedone finiscono nella stessa scatola."

When you finish the game, the king and pawn end up in the same box."

What does the proverb mean?

All human beings ultimately share the same fate.

Why chess?

metaphor for life.

The king and the pawn.

The philosophical lesson

success should be approached with perspective.

Why has the saying survived for so long?

Every generation faces the same temptation to equate status with worth.

Contemporary relevance

The billionaire, the office worker, the politician and the student all share a common humanity.

Examples in everyday life

A lesson for everyone

he king and the pawn return to the same box.

Translated into English, the proverb reads: "At first glance, it sounds like a simple observation about chess. After all, when a game ends, every piece is returned to the same container. The king, the queen, the bishops, the knights and the pawns are all packed away together.Yet for centuries, people have found a deeper meaning in this image. The proverb has endured because it captures one of the most fundamental truths about human life: status, wealth and power may separate people during their lives, but they do not separate them forever.In chess, the king is the most important piece on the board. The pawn is the least powerful. One commands attention. The other is more or less sacrificed.The proverb uses this contrast to represent society itself. Kings, rulers, executives and celebrities occupy positions of influence. Ordinary workers, labourers and citizens often have far less power.But once the game is over, the distinction, the difference, the contrast disappears.The message is simple:No amount of money, rank or influence can exempt anyone from mortality. In the end, the king and the pawn return to the same box.Chess has long been used as aThe game contains hierarchy, strategy, conflict, sacrifice and ambition. Different pieces have different abilities and different levels of importance. Because of this, chess provides a natural way to reflect on social structures and human behaviour.The proverb focuses on two opposite figures:The king symbolises authority, privilege and power. The pawn symbolises ordinary people. By placing them in the same box at the end, the proverb reminds listeners that social distinctions are temporary rather than permanent.At its heart, the proverb is about humility.Many philosophical traditions, from ancient Greece to Eastern thought, have stressed the importance of recognising the limits of power. Human achievements may be impressive, but they do not make anyone immune to ageing, illness or death.This does not mean success is meaningless. Rather, it suggests thatA powerful leader may command nations. A wealthy entrepreneur may build a global business. A famous athlete may inspire millions. Yet all remain human beings with the same basic vulnerabilities as everyone else.The proverb encourages people to remember that fact.The reason is straightforward:History is filled with rulers who believed their power would last forever. Empires rose and fell. Dynasties flourished and disappeared. Vast fortunes were accumulated and lost.The proverb acts as a corrective to that illusion.It reminds people that a person's value should not be measured solely by wealth, titles or influence. Character, integrity and how one treats others often leave a more lasting impression than status alone.Despite originating in a world very different from our own, the proverb feels remarkably modern.Today's society is often driven by rankings and comparisons. Social media platforms display follower counts. Businesses publish wealth rankings. Celebrities are elevated to near-mythical status. Success is constantly measured and displayed.Against that backdrop, the proverb offers a counterpoint.It suggests that while achievement matters, it should not become the sole measure of a human life.The saying also resonates in workplaces. Effective leaders are often those who understand that titles do not make them inherently superior to the people they manage. Respect is earned through actions, not merely position.Consider a successful chief executive who treats employees with dignity and respect. Such a leader understands that authority is temporary and that every person deserves consideration.Or think of a retired public figure. Once surrounded by attention and influence, they eventually return to a quieter life, much like everyone else.The proverb does not deny differences in achievement. Instead, it encourages people not to confuse achievement with intrinsic worth.One reason the saying remains popular is that it speaks to both the powerful and the powerless.For those in positions of authority, it is a reminder to stay humble.For those without power, it is a reminder that status does not determine human value.Both lessons remain relevant in every society.The image of a king and a pawn resting side by side in a box after the game has ended is powerful precisely because it is so simple. No lengthy explanation is required. The symbolism is immediately clear.The board may contain winners and losers. Life may contain privilege and hardship. But eventually, the game ends for everyone.And when it does, t
Article Summary: A pawn can also defeat a king in a game of chess. When you finish the game, the king and pawn end up in the same box. What does the proverb mean? All human beings ultimately share the same fate. The philosophical lesson is that success should be approached with perspective.

Article Title: Japanese proverb of the day: Fall seven times, stand up eight; life lessons on failure, courage, success and why winners are people who refuse to stay down
Article Text:
Fall seven times, stand up eight - Japanese proverb of the day

"Fall seven times, stand up eight" — Japanese Proverb

Proverb of the Day

The deeper meaning behind the proverb

Why this lesson matters today

The takeaway

Nana korobi, ya oki – the famous Japanese proverb that translates to "fall down seven times, get up eight." – is a reminder that resilience is not about avoiding failure, but about refusing to let it have the final word.To succeed does not mean to get to your goal in one try, it means to keep trying till you get to your goal. A centuries-old Japanese proverb captures this idea perfectly, and in June 2026, the New York Knicks wrote it into history.The proverb offers a powerful reminder that success is rarely the result of one dramatic effort. More often, it is the outcome of getting back up, time after time, even when defeat seems certain. Like an athlete who keeps pushing through exhaustion, steady determination can overcome obstacles that may initially appear impossible. The quote highlights a simple truth: lasting achievements are often built through resilience and the refusal to stay down rather than sheer force or talent alone.Life will knock you down; that is inevitable. Yet over years, decades and a lifetime, those who keep getting back up gradually shape their own destinies, carving paths through obstacles that once seemed insurmountable.The proverb uses this human experience as a metaphor for success and is synonymous to relentlessness, momentum and adaptability. Talent, intelligence and resources can be valuable, but they are often outmatched by determination. People who continue moving forward despite setbacks frequently achieve results that once seemed impossible.In an age of instant gratification, resilience can feel like an old-fashioned virtue. Social media often highlights overnight success stories, but most achievements happen slowly and quietly. Whether it is preparing for an examination, building a business, improving health or mastering a skill, meaningful progress usually comes from the refusal to give up. Small actions performed consistently can create transformative results over time.Many inventors, athletes, artists and entrepreneurs faced repeated failures before succeeding. One of the most powerful modern examples of resilience is the story of the 2026 New York Knicks.In June 2026, the New York Knicks won their first NBA championship in 53 years. It was not a dominant run; it was a run of comebacks. The Knicks trailed by double digits in all five games of the Finals. In Game 4, they were down by 29 points—the largest comeback in NBA Finals history. In Game 5, they were down by 16 before rallying to win the title. They finished the playoffs 6-2 in games they trailed by double digits, the best such record in NBA history.Jalen Brunson, the Finals MVP, was the architect of these comebacks. His mindset comes from a simple phrase his parents taught him: "Never be afraid to fail.""I've always told myself and always been taught by my parents, never be afraid to fail," Brunson said during the Finals. "You put yourself in those positions when you're in the summertime, when you're envisioning what's going on on the court, when you're by yourself on the court. When those opportunities come about, you're not afraid of that moment 'cause you worked hard enough to where if you do fail, you're gonna learn anyway. "That is the proverb in action: fall seven times, stand up eight. The Knicks fell behind in every game. They got back up in every game. In the end, they were champions.The next time a goal feels too difficult or progress seems too slow, remember the proverb. Fall seven times, stand up eight. Strength may create impact, but resilience creates change. Sometimes, the most powerful force in the world is simply refusing to give up.
Article Summary: Japanese proverb nana korobi, ya oki translates to "fall down seven times, get up eight" in june 2026, the new york knicks won their first nba championship in 53 years. The proverb offers a powerful reminder that success is rarely the result of one dramatic effort.

Article Title: Peruvian proverb of the day: 'Little by little, one walks far' — life lessons on patience, discipline, success and why small daily efforts create big results
Article Text:
Proverb of the day: 'Little by little, one walks far' — Why consistency beats talent in the long run (AI-generated image)

"Little by little, one walks far" — Peruvian Proverb

Proverb of the day

The deeper meaning behind the proverb

Why this lesson matters today

The science behind small progress

The takeaway

"Poco a poco se anda lejos" — a traditional Peruvian proverb that translates to 'Little by little, one walks far' — offers a timeless reminder that meaningful achievements are rarely built overnight.Whether the goal is personal growth, career success, financial stability or mastering a skill, lasting progress often comes through steady effort rather than dramatic leaps.In a world that celebrates instant results, viral success and overnight transformations, this simple proverb encourages a different perspective: trust the process, keep moving forward and let consistency do the work.The proverb highlights a truth that many people overlook. Great accomplishments are usually the result of small actions repeated over long periods of time. A single step may seem insignificant, but thousands of steps can carry a person across mountains, countries and even entire careers.The saying teaches patience without encouraging passivity. It does not suggest waiting for success to arrive. Instead, it emphasises continuous movement. Even slow progress remains progress if it moves in the right direction.Many people abandon their goals because they focus on how far they still have to go.The proverb shifts attention to the next step rather than the final destination. Over weeks, months and years, those small steps accumulate into extraordinary results.At its heart, the proverb is about consistency.People often underestimate what they can achieve through modest daily efforts. Reading ten pages a day can become dozens of books a year. Saving a small amount regularly can grow into significant financial security. Exercising for a short period every day can transform health over time.The proverb also reflects an important principle found in many successful individuals: discipline is often more powerful than motivation. Motivation comes and goes, but consistent habits continue even on difficult days.Modern society often rewards speed, yet some of the most valuable achievements require patience. Education, relationships, careers and personal development are all long journeys. The proverb reminds us that slow progress is not failure. In many cases, it is the most sustainable path to success.The message is especially relevant in an age dominated by social media.People are constantly exposed to carefully curated images of success, creating the impression that achievements happen instantly. What is rarely visible are the years of preparation, setbacks, learning and persistence that usually occur behind the scenes.A modern example of this principle can be found in the career of Novak Djokovic.Widely regarded as one of the greatest tennis players in history, Djokovic's success was not built in a single season. It came through years of disciplined training, continuous improvement and relentless consistency. Long before he became a record-breaking champion, he spent countless hours refining his game, recovering from setbacks and making incremental improvements.His achievements illustrate the essence of the proverb. Championships are won on match day, but they are built through thousands of small efforts long before the trophy is lifted.The same principle applies beyond sport. Entrepreneurs build businesses one decision at a time. Writers complete books one page at a time. Students earn degrees one lesson at a time. Significant achievements often emerge from ordinary actions repeated consistently.Psychologists have long observed that incremental improvements can create powerful long-term outcomes.The idea is sometimes referred to as the "compound effect" — the principle that small actions, repeated consistently, produce results that grow over time. Just as compound interest can transform modest savings into substantial wealth, daily habits can gradually reshape skills, knowledge and behaviour.What feels insignificant today may become transformative in the future.The next time a goal feels overwhelming or progress seems too slow, remember the Peruvian proverb: "Little by little, one walks far."Success does not always belong to the fastest person. More often, it belongs to the one who keeps moving.Small efforts may not seem powerful in the moment, but over time they become the foundation of remarkable achievements. Patience, discipline and consistency rarely make headlines, yet they remain among the most reliable paths to lasting success.Sometimes, the greatest journey begins with the smallest step.
Article Summary: Proverb of the day: 'little by little, one walks far' — why consistency beats talent in the long run. "poco a poco se anda lejos" is a traditional peruvian proverb that translates to 'little by little. One walk far' it offers a timeless reminder that meaningful achievements are rarely built overnight.

The winning numbers for the Powerball draw held on 20 June 2026 have been officially announced, with a staggering jackpot of $302 million up for grabs. As one of the largest lottery games in the United States, Powerball continues to draw millions of participants who dream of winning a life-changing fortune. The growing jackpot generated significant excitement ahead of the draw, prompting players across the country to purchase tickets in hopes of matching the winning combination.Powerball has long been known for producing massive jackpots that capture nationwide attention, and the latest draw was no exception. The announcement of the winning numbers marks the beginning of the claims process, as officials verify results and determine whether a lucky winner has emerged. If no ticket matches all the required numbers, the jackpot could roll over once again, creating an even larger prize for the next draw. For now, all eyes remain on the latest results and the possibility of a new multimillionaire being crowned.Powerball is one of the most popular lottery games in the United States, offering players the opportunity to win cash prizes ranging from smaller payouts to enormous jackpots that often climb into the hundreds of millions—or even billions—of dollars.The game is operated by the Multi-State Lottery Association (MUSL) and is available across numerous U.S. states, making it a nationwide phenomenon that attracts millions of participants for every draw.To play, participants select five main numbers, known as the white balls, from a pool of 1 to 69. They must then choose one additional number, the Powerball, from a separate set ranging from 1 to 26. The winning numbers are determined through officially supervised random drawings, conducted using certified lottery machines or approved electronic systems to ensure fairness and complete randomness. Since every draw is independent of previous results, each ticket has an equal chance of winning.Powerball drawings are held three times each week every Monday, Wednesday, and Saturday at 10:59 PM Eastern Time giving players multiple chances throughout the week to compete for some of the largest lottery prizes in the world.Start by selecting five numbers ranging from 1 to 69. These numbers make up your primary combination for the draw.Next, choose one additional number between 1 and 26. This red Powerball is crucial, as matching it can significantly impact the amount you win.If you'd rather leave the selection to chance, choose Quick Pick. The system will automatically generate all six numbers randomly for your ticket.Complete your purchase before ticket sales close for the upcoming Powerball draw to ensure your entry is valid.After buying your ticket, you are automatically entered into the next scheduled live drawing.Once the winning numbers are announced, compare them with your ticket to find out whether you've won a prize.For an extra $1 per play, you can include the Power Play feature, which multiplies eligible non-jackpot winnings by a randomly selected multiplier, potentially increasing your payout.The winning numbers were 16, 20, 44, 48, 50, with the Powerball number 15. The Power Play multiplier was 2x.The estimated jackpot for the draw was $302 million, making it one of the largest lottery prizes available at the time.Powerball drawings take place three times a week—every Monday, Wednesday, and Saturday at 10:59 PM Eastern Time.Power Play is an optional add-on that costs an extra $1 per play and can multiply eligible non-jackpot prizes by a randomly selected multiplier.If no ticket matches all five white balls and the Powerball, the jackpot rolls over to the next draw, increasing the prize amount for future players.

Article Title: Shattering records and cultivating future stars
Article Text:
Mayura Shanbaug

Ashish Kumar Srivastava - MD and CEO of PNB MetLife

Sameer Bansal - Chief Distribution Officer of PNB MetLife

Chirag Shetty - Asian Games Gold Medalist & JBC 2023 mentor

Satwiksairaj Rankireddy - Asian Games Gold Medalist & JBC 2023 mentor

Disclaimer: Content Produced by PNB Metlife

The 7th PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship shattered records with over 8,200 young talent nationwide, earning global recognition and fostering a culture of resilience and determination in aspiring badminton starsIn a spectacular display of badminton prowess and youthful enthusiasm, the 7th edition of the PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship (JBC) etched its name in the annals of sporting history. Celebrating a staggering participation of 8257 young champions from across India, the championship not only broke its own record set in 2022 but also clinched the title of the ‘Most kids participating in a Badminton Championship in multiple cities’ as recognised by the World Record Certification Agency (WRCA).The grand finale unfolded at the Indira Gandhi Indoor Stadium in New Delhi, where the air was thick with competition, camaraderie, and the unmistakable passion for badminton. The event, graced by chief guests Chirag Shetty and Satwik Rankireddy, Asian Games Gold Medalists and JBC 2023 mentors, was a true celebration of the sport’s rising stars.The 10-city extravaganza, spanning the length and breadth of the country, showcased the unwavering commitment of PNB MetLife in nurturing young talents in the badminton arena. With participants exceeding 8200, it’s evident that the PNB MetLife JBC has become a beacon for aspiring shuttlers, highlighting the deep-seated passion for badminton among the diverse and talented youth of India.Beyond the glitz and glamour of the record-breaking numbers, the championship holds a special place in the hearts of those at the helm. Ashish Kumar Srivastava, MD and CEO of PNB MetLife, expressed pride in not just surpassing their own world record but also in contributing to India’s glory on the international badminton stage. The championship, he emphasized, is not merely a competition but a significant milestone in creating a pan-India badminton ecosystem.This edition of the JBC saw a strategic partnership with world champions Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, who served as mentors for the young talents. Sameer Bansal, Chief Distribution Officer of PNB MetLife, acknowledged the pivotal role played by these champions in inspiring the next generation of badminton stars. The focus, according to Bansal, is on unleashing the latent potential of young shuttlers and preparing them for global excellence.The Delhi chapter of the championship witnessed some nail-biting action with over 1000 participants competing fiercely across various categories. From Ananya Talyan’s stellar performance in Girls Singles Under 9 to Divyansh Rawat’s triumph in Boys Singles Under 17, the young talents left no shuttle unturned in their quest for victory.The results from the Delhi leg not only showcased the raw talent but also highlighted the championship’s commitment to breaking barriers. The PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship is not just about records; it’s about fostering a culture of healthy living, determination, and resilience among the future champions of the sport.In the words of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, the championship serves as an invaluable platform.---------------------------------------------------------------As we celebrate the successful culmination of the 7th edition of PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship , we take pride in surpassing our own world record for highest participation and for being recognized by World Record Certification Agency. Beyond the records, JBC represents a significant milestone in our mission to lay the groundwork for India’ s glory and success on the international badminton stage by creating a pan-India badminton ecosystem that nurtures champions at the grassroots levelThis year has been especially remarkable for PNB MetLife JBC as we joined hands with world champions Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy as JBC Mentors for the young talents associated with this tournament. They have played a pivotal role in inspiring young shuttlers to dream big and elevate their game and skills. We are eager for the next edition to unfold in our journey of unveiling the latent badminton potential across our nation and preparing them for global excellenceIt’s inspiring to witness the boundless talent and enthusiasm within our young athletes. My journey as JBC mentor has been incredibly fulfilling, as I got the opportunity to guide these budding champions and witness the journey of these young badminton stars. This championship is more than a competition; it is a platform that nurtures dreams, ignites aspirations, and, I believe, it is laying the foundation for future gold medalists in the makingTournaments like these serve as an invaluable platform for nurturing and harnessing the potential of our country’s young badminton stars. Witnessing the enthusiasm, passion, and skill displayed by these emerging shuttlers reinforces the belief that our country has an immense reservoir of talent. My association as a JBC mentor has been a source of immense pride, particularly given the role that the championship is playing in creating an incredible platform that is serving as a powerful catalyst, empowering young players and bolstering their confidence to become future champions
Article Summary: The 7th pnb metlife junior badminton championship shattered records with over 8,200 young talent nationwide. The grand finale unfolded at the indira gandhi indoor stadium in new delhi, where the air was thick with competition, camaraderie, and the unmistakable passion for badminton.

Article Title: Nodwin Gaming unveils India’s ENC 2026 roster for League of Legends
Article Text:
India’s lineup



ENC 2026 format



India’s wider ENC campaign



Nodwin Gaming has unveiled India’s official League of Legends roster for the Esports Nations Cup (ENC) 2026, set to be held in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, from November 2 to 29. The team will compete in the regional qualifiers from June 19–21 for a chance to secure a spot at the main event, which features 32 national teams and a prize pool exceeding Rs 14 crore.The Indian squad will be led by Aakash Shandilya (Infi), one of Asia’s most decorated junglers with over 35 tournament victories. Joining him are:* Akshaj Shenoy (Kat Bot) – a consistent top performer and key figure in India’s competitive scene.* Rahul Bisht (Bob) – regarded as India’s leading support player, known for his game-changing presence.* Vinayak Gupta (Panda) – India’s premier top laner, adding strength and familiarity to the lineup.* Niranjan Prabakaran (Sam) – a reliable player known for consistency and team-oriented play.Substitutes include Sharavan Kumar (DoubleYou), valued for his versatility, and Manoj Ravish (Shockya), one of India’s leading bot laners. The team will be coached by Pankaj Upadhyay (KAKA).Infi and Kat Bot were part of India’s League of Legends contingent at the Hangzhou Asian Games 2023, where the team finished fifth.Alongside Bob, they currently represent S8UL Esports and have also qualified for the upcoming 20th Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya, giving the national team a strong, experienced core.Speaking on the announcement, Infi said: “Representing India is always a special responsibility. This roster brings together players who have competed together for years and understand each other’s strengths well. We have a strong mix of experience, individual talent, and synergy, and our immediate focus is on preparing well for the qualifiers and earning our place at the Esports Nations Cup.”The Esports Nations Cup will feature 32 national teams competing for USD 1.5 million (~INR 14 crore). Sixteen teams will earn direct invitations based on ENC rankings, while 14 will qualify through regional online tournaments. Two additional slots will be awarded via wildcard entries.India’s preparations extend across multiple titles:* DOTA 2 – roster led by Krish Gupta, competing in qualifiers June 29–July 1.* Chess – Grandmasters Nihal Sarin and Mitrabha Guha have secured direct qualification.* MOBA Legends: 5v5! – team led by Kyle, currently ranked 11th globally.* Rocket League – roster led by Abscrazy, competing in qualifiers July 17–19.
Article Summary: Nodwin gaming has unveiled india’s official league of legends roster for the esports nations cup (enc) 2026. The team will compete in the regional qualifiers from june 19–21 for a chance to secure a spot at the main event, which features 32 national teams and a prize pool exceeding rs 14 crore.

Article Title: Forza Horizon 6 'Blast from the Past': How to find and win this weekly challenge
Article Text:
Image via Playground Games

How to complete all Blast from the Past Weekly Challenge tasks

Task 1: Get the 1982 DeLorean DMC-12

Task 2: Maintain a speed of 88 MPH or above for 10 seconds

Task 3: Take a photo of the DeLorean

Win a Road Race to finish the challenge

Forza Horizon 6's "Blast from the Past" Weekly Challenge is now live. Players looking to complete this week's challenge will need to finish four objectives focused on the 1982 DeLorean DMC-12. The challenge appears in the Summer section of the Festival Playlist and becomes available after completing the Rookie Yellow Wristband event in the main story.To complete the challenge, players must obtain the DeLorean, maintain a specific speed, take a photo of the vehicle, and win a Road Race while driving it. Finishing all four objectives will complete the weekly challenge.The first objective is to acquire the 1982 DeLorean DMC-12. Players can buy the vehicle from the AutoShow by opening the Cars tab and selecting "Buy New and Used Cars."The DeLorean has its own manufacturer category and costs 72,000 Credits. After purchasing it, drive the car a short distance to complete the first objective.The second objective requires players to keep the DeLorean travelling at 88 MPH or faster for 10 consecutive seconds, a clear nod to the Back to the Future franchise.A good location for this challenge is the Festival Kilometer Drag Meet in the Ohtani region. The long straight road provides ample space to maintain the required speed without slowing. Stay above 88 MPH for 10 seconds, and the objective should complete automatically.The third objective is simple. Enter Photo Mode and take a picture of the 1982 DeLorean DMC-12. Players can access Photo Mode by pressing the D-pad down or the P key on a keyboard. The photo does not need a high score; the DeLorean only needs to be visible in the image.The final objective requires players to win any Road Race while driving the 1982 DeLorean DMC-12. A convenient option is the Tokyo Railway Sprint Road Race, located northeast of Tokyo City near the Minamino region. As one of the earlier races available in the game, it is often a suitable choice for completing this requirement.If winning proves difficult, players can create a custom event from a previously completed race and reduce the difficulty setting to Tourist. Depending on the selected race, it may also be necessary to adjust the event theme so that the DeLorean is eligible to participate. Once players win a Road Race in the DeLorean, the fourth and final objective will be completed, finishing the Blast from the Past Weekly Challenge.
Article Summary: Forza horizon 6's "blast from the past" weekly challenge is now live. Players looking to complete this week's challenge will need to finish four objectives focused on the 1982 delorean dmc-12. The challenge appears in the summer section of the festival playlist and becomes available after completing the rookie yellow wristband event in the main story.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scores fastest List A fifty, but 324.14 strike rate still isn't his best
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Image credit: ICC)

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi once again showcased why he is regarded as one of India's brightest young talents, producing a breathtaking 94 off just 29 balls against Sri Lanka A in the Tri-Nation Series final. The 15-year-old struck at an incredible rate of 324.14, hammering 10 fours and 8 sixes before falling 6-run short of a century. While this innings ranks among the most destructive ever played for India A in the 50-over format, it was not the fastest-scoring knock of his young career.The 15-year-old opener unleashed a breathtaking assault on the Sri Lankan bowlers after Sri Lanka A elected to field first, racing to his half-century in just 11 deliveries, conjuring up a stunning sequence of 4, 4, 4, 6, 6, 0, 6, 4, 4, 6, 6 off his first 11 deliveries. In doing so, Sooryavanshi shattered the previous world record of a 12-ball fifty, set by Sri Lanka's Kaushalya Weeraratne in 2005.But this was only his second-best innings in terms of strike rate. The best came in November 2025 when, at just 14 years and 232 days old, the left-handed opener rewrote the record books with a remarkable 144 off 42 balls against the UAE in the Asia Cup Rising Stars tournament.In that T20 match, he smashed 11 fours and 15 sixes, scoring at a staggering strike rate of 342.86.He reached his century in just 32 balls, making it the joint third-fastest T20 century by an Indian batter with Rishabh Pant who smashes a 32 ball hundred for Delhi in SMAT . Sooryavanshi powered India A to a monumental 297/4 in their 20 overs and played a key role in steering the side to an emphatic 148-run victory.Although Vaibhav Sooyavanshi's highest run for Team India came in U19-Final match against England in 2026. Vaibhav dismantled England's bowling attack with 15 fours and 15 sixes and scored 175 runs off just 80 balls at a strike rate of 218.75 and powering India to a record 411/9 in the final.
Article Summary: Vaibhav sooryavanshi smashed 94 off just 29 balls against sri lanka a. The 15-year-old struck at an incredible rate of 324.14, hammering 10 fours and 8 sixes before falling 6-run short of a century. He shattered the previous world record of a 12-ball fifty, set by sri lanka's kaushalya weeraratne in 2005.
